headlines.description: A late goal by new signing Joshua Zirkzee earned Manchester United a nervy 1-0 victory over Fulham in their Premier League season opener at Old Trafford on Friday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Zirkzee debut goal gives Man Utd win in PL opener

headlines.description: Second-half goals from Diogo Jota and Mohamed Salah allowed new Liverpool coach Arne Slot to get off to a 2-0 winning start at newly promoted Ipswich Town in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah strikes as Slot opens Liverpool era with win

headlines.description: Arsenal eased to a 2-0 win at home to Wolverhampton Wanderers, with goals by Kai Havertz and Bukayo Saka getting their latest Premier League title challenge up and running on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saka, Havertz goals start Arsenal title tilt with win

headlines.description: Danny Welbeck scored one goal and set up another as Brighton & Hove Albion eased to a 3-0 victory over a Everton on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton win as Milner starts record 23rd season

headlines.description: Joelinton's goal earned Newcastle a 1-0 win over promoted Southampton in their first game of the Premier League season at St James' Park, despite going down to 10 men in the first half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ten-man Newcastle down Southampton in opener

headlines.description: Bournemouth forward Antoine Semenyo rescued a point for his side with a late equaliser as they drew 1-1 at Nottingham Forest in the Premier League on Saturday in a game marred by a serious-looking injury to Forest's Brazilian midfielder Danilo.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest-Bournemouth draw marred by Danilo injury

name: Aston Villa at West Ham United

headlines.description: Amadou Onana scored four minutes into his Aston Villa debut and Jhon Duran netted the late winner as Unai Emery's men kicked off their Premier League season with a 2-1 victory at West Ham United on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Onana, Duran earn Villa win against West Ham

headlines.description: Yoane Wissa's close-range finish gave Brentford a winning start to their Premier League campaign with a hard-fought 2-1 home victory over Crystal Palace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Toney-less Brentford beat Palace to start season

headlines.description: Erling Haaland scored his 91st goal in his 100th appearance for Manchester City on Sunday as the champions kicked off their Premier League title defence with a lively 2-0 away win over Chelsea.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland helps City down Chelsea to begin season

headlines.description: Veteran striker Jamie Vardy earned newly-promoted Leicester City a 1-1 draw with a profligate Tottenham Hotspur in their Premier League season opener on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leicester draw with Tottenham in return to EPL

name: Manchester United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Table toppers Brighton beat Manchester United 2-1 on Saturday to stay undefeated in the Premier League, with João Pedro scoring the winner in the fifth minute of added time.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton snatch late win over Man United

name: West Ham United at Crystal Palace

headlines.description: A pair of second-half goals from Tomas Soucek and Jarrod Bowen gave West Ham United their first Premier League win of the season as they cruised to a 2-0 victory over Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham punish Palace with 2-0 Premier League win

headlines.description: Goals from Emile Smith Rowe and Alex Iwobi gave Fulham a 2-1 home victory over promoted Leicester City in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham beat Leicester for first points of season

headlines.description: Manchester City scored a flurry of three goals in less than four minutes to beat newly-promoted Ipswich Town 4-1 in the Premier League on Saturday, roaring back from a shock start at Etihad Stadium.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland nets hat trick as Man City's rout Ipswich

headlines.description: Newly-promoted Southampton's difficult start to life back in the Premier League continued when they slipped to a 1-0 loss at home to Nottingham Forest on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest earn first win of season over Southampton

headlines.description: Goalkeeper Jordan Pickford's error and Son Heung-min's brace helped Tottenham Hotspur to a comprehensive 4-0 victory over hapless Everton in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Son double eases Spurs to 4-0 win over Everton

headlines.description: Second-half goals by Leandro Trossard and Thomas Partey earned Arsenal earn a hard-fought 2-0 win at Aston Villa on Saturday as Mikel Arteta's side continued their perfect start to the Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Trossard, Raya help Arsenal get revenge on Villa

headlines.description: Newcastle United's Anthony Gordon equalised 14 minutes from time to secure a 1-1 draw at Bournemouth in the Premier League on Sunday and spoil their hosts' 125th anniversary celebrations.
headlines.shortLinkText: VAR denies Bounemouth winner vs. Newcastle

headlines.description: Noni Madueke scored a second half hat trick as Chelsea crushed Wolverhampton Wanderers 6-2 away in a frenetic Premier League clash at Molineux on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea win 6-2 as Madueke nets hat trick

headlines.description: Arne Slot's comfortable start to life as Liverpool manager continued when his new side strolled to a 2-0 victory over Brentford in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah nets as Liverpool keep up winning start

headlines.description: Brighton held Arsenal to a 1-1 draw at the Emirates on Saturday after Declan Rice was controversially sent off in the second half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rice red card thwarts Arsenal in draw vs. Brighton

headlines.description: Bryan Mbeumo's brace helped Brentford to a comfortable 3-1 home win over promoted Southampton in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford comfortably beat sorry Southampton

headlines.description: Bournemouth scored three late goals to stun Everton 3-2 in the Premier League at Goodison Park and leave the hosts without a point from three matches.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton stunned by late Bournemouth comeback

headlines.description: Liam Delap's goal was enough to earn Ipswich their first Premier League point of the season, but they had to share the spoils in a 1-1 draw with Fulham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Draw with Fulham sees Ipswich earn first PL point

headlines.description: Aston Villa returned to winning ways in the Premier League as goals by Amadou Onana and Jhon Duran wrapped up a 2-1 victory at Leicester City on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa back on track with win at Leicester

headlines.description: Jean-Ricner Bellegarde's stunning strike earned Wolves their first point of the Premier League season with a 1-1 draw at Nottingham Forest on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves, Forest draw despite Bellegarde stunner

name: Manchester City at West Ham United

headlines.description: Erling Haaland's second straight hat trick propelled Manchester City to a 3-1 win at West Ham, maintaining the champions' perfect start to the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland scores 2nd straight hat trick in City win

headlines.description: Chelsea forward Nicholas Jackson opened the scoring on 25 minutes, but a second-half equaliser by Eberechi Eze earned Crystal Palace a 1-1 draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jackson nets but Chelsea held to draw with Palace

headlines.description: Alexander Isak scored the winner for Newcastle United as they weathered a storm from visiting Tottenham to secure a 2-1 win on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Isak goal sees Newcastle snatch win over Spurs

headlines.description: Two goals from Luis Diaz and one from Mohamed Salah helped Liverpool stroll to a 3-0 victory at Manchester United in the Premier League on Sunday, with Erik ten Hag's side humbled by their fierce rivals.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool humble rivals Man United in 3-0 win

headlines.description: Marcus Rashford scored for the first time since March in Manchester United's 3-0 Premier League victory at Southampton on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rashford on target as Utd win 3-0 at St. Mary's

name: Ipswich Town at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton kept up their unbeaten start to the season but dropped their first home points as a plucky Ipswich Town held out for a fortunate goalless draw in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Plucky Ipswich earn draw at unbeaten Brighton

headlines.description: Crystal Palace's Jean-Philippe Mateta scored twice including a last-gasp penalty as they clawed their way back from two goals down to salvage a 2-2 Premier League draw against promoted Leicester City at Selhurst Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Mateta penalty saves Palace against Leicester

headlines.description: A late Danny Ings goal snatched an unlikely 1-1 draw for West Ham United at Fulham in their Premier League derby at Craven Cottage on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ings scores late as West Ham draw with Fulham

headlines.description: A superb second-half goal from substitute Callum Hudson-Odoi gave Nottingham Forest the biggest surprise win of the Premier League season so far as they beat Liverpool 1-0 at Anfield on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool stunned by Forest at Anfield for 1st loss

headlines.description: Manchester City beat Brentford 2-1 at home on Saturday to maintain their perfect start to the Premier League season after Erling Haaland netted twice following the fastest goal of the season from the visitors.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland strikes twice as Man City come from behind to beat Brentford

headlines.description: Jhon Duran scored a brilliant long-range winner as Aston Villa came from two goals down to beat Everton 3-2 in a hugely entertaining Premier League clash on Saturday, as striker Ollie Watkins opened his account for the season with a brace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Duran stunner sees Villa edge Everton in thriller

headlines.description: Chelsea substitute Christopher Nkunku struck in the 86th minute to secure a 1-0 victory at Bournemouth in a game littered with a Premier League record 14 yellow cards on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sub Nkunku nets late Chelsea winner at B'mouth

headlines.description: Gabriel's second-half header earned Arsenal a crucial 1-0 win at Tottenham Hotspur in a feisty North London derby on Sunday to keep pace with early Premier League leaders Manchester City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gabriel earns Arsenal win at Spurs in feisty derby

headlines.description: Newcastle United fought back from a halftime deficit with two long-range strikes in the space of five minutes to beat Wolverhampton Wanderers 2-1 at Molineux on Sunday and move into third place in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle orchestrate late rally to down Wolves

headlines.description: Nicolas Jackson scored twice to help Chelsea to a third straight away win with a 3-0 victory at London rivals West Ham United on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jackson, Palmer score as Chelsea beat West Ham

headlines.description: Aston Villa denied Wolverhampton Wanderers their first victory in the Premier League this season after they came from behind to win their West Midlands derby.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa score late to deny Wolves first points

headlines.description: Fulham's Raul Jimenez, Emile Smith Rowe and Reiss Nelson all scored in a 3-1 victory over Newcastle United at Craven Cottage on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham beat Newcastle 3-1 to hand Magpies first defeat of season

headlines.description: Struggling Everton missed the chance to win their first Premier League game of the season when they were held to a 1-1 draw at promoted Leicester City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton take first point of season in Leicester draw

headlines.description: A quick-fire double from Luis Diaz set Liverpool on course for a comprehensive 3-0 Premier League home win against Bournemouth on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Diaz double helps Liverpool beat Bournemouth

headlines.description: Sam Morsy's deflected shot after 95 minutes rescued a point for Ipswich Town in a 1-1 draw with Southampton at St Mary's Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Morsy strike earns Ipswich 1-1 draw at Southampton

headlines.description: Striker Dominic Solanke scored his first goal for Tottenham Hotspur as Ange Postecoglou's team recovered from conceding inside the opening minute to beat Brentford 3-1 in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Solanke scores first Spurs goal in Brentford win

headlines.description: Manchester United had to settle for a single point despite a dominant performance by Erik ten Hag's team in their 0-0 Premier League draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United rue missed chances in draw at Palace

name: Nottingham Forest at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton and Nottingham Forest maintained their unbeaten starts to the Premier League season with a topsy-turvy 2-2 draw.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton and Forest slug out eventful 2-2 draw

headlines.description: John Stones scored deep in injury time as Manchester City salvaged a 2-2 draw with 10-man Arsenal on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: City stun 10-man Arsenal with 98th-minute goal

headlines.description: Man City were held to a second straight draw in the Premier League after Newcastle's Anthony Gordon equaliser earned a point for the home side.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodri-less Man City drop points in Newcastle draw

headlines.description: Leandro Trossard erased the memory of his red card last week as he played a crucial role in Arsenal's 4-2 Premier League win over Leicester City after the Londoners had somehow squandered a two-goal lead on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal score two stoppage-time goals to beat Leicester

headlines.description: Tomas Soucek cancelled out Bryan Mbeumo's opener as West Ham United drew 1-1 at Brentford on Saturday, with the hosts becoming the first team in Premier League history to score in the opening minute in three successive matches.
headlines.shortLinkText: Soucek saves point for West Ham at Brentford

headlines.description: Chelsea's Cole Palmer scored four first-half goals, including a stunning long-range free kick and a penalty, in a breathless 4-2 Premier League victory over Brighton on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palmer makes history in Chelsea win vs. Brighton

headlines.description: Everton earned their first Premier League win of the season as a double from Dwight McNeil saw them recover from an early deficit to beat Crystal Palace 2-1 at Goodison Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: McNeil double earns Everton win over Palace

headlines.description: Fulham forward Raul Jimenez converted a second-half penalty to give Marco Silva's side a 1-0 win at Nottingham Forest in the Premier League on Saturday, handing the hosts their first defeat of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jimenez penalty gives Fulham 1-0 win at Forest

headlines.description: Ibrahima Konate headed home his first ever Premier League goal and Mohamed Salah converted a penalty as Liverpool climbed to the top of the Premier League table with a 2-1 victory over last-place Wolverhampton Wanderers on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool top Premier League with win over Wolves

headlines.description: Ipswich Town drew 2-2 with Aston Villa in the Premier League on Sunday, as Liam Delap scored a brace for the home side at Portman Road.
headlines.shortLinkText: Delap double earns Ipswich draw with Aston Villa

headlines.description: Tottenham's Brennan Johnson, Dejan Kulusevski and Dominic Solanke got on the scoresheet in a 3-0 victory over 10-man Manchester United on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fernandes sees red as Man Utd trounced by Spurs

headlines.description: Bournemouth winger Antoine Semenyo scored one first-half goal and played a big part in creating another to set his side on their way to a convincing 3-1 home win over struggling Southampton in the Premier League on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo sees Bournemouth to win over Southampton

headlines.description: Diogo Jota's early goal was enough to give Liverpool a tight 1-0 victory at Crystal Palace on Saturday and ensure the Reds go into the international break top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool beat Palace to retain Prem top spot

headlines.description: Arsenal bounced back from conceding a goal to lowly Southampton to win 3-1 after a pulsating second half in their Premier League clash.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saka steers Arsenal to win over Southampton

headlines.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers boss Gary O'Neil took "full responsibility" after Brentford registered a thumping 5-3 win over his beleaguered side in the Premier League on Saturday to leave them winless and bottom of the table after seven games.
headlines.shortLinkText: O'Neil takes 'full responsibility' as Wolves remain winless in Premier League

headlines.description: Facundo Buonanotte's goal lifted Leicester City to their first win of the Premier League season as they beat Bournemouth 1-0 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leicester beat Bournemouth 1-0 for first win of season

headlines.description: Mateo Kovacic scored either side of halftime to fire Manchester City to an entertaining 3-2 victory over Fulham at a sun-drenched Etihad Stadium on Saturday, as the hosts kept their grip on second place in the Premier League standings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Kovacic brace gives Man City victory over Fulham

name: Ipswich Town at West Ham United

headlines.description: West Ham United claimed their first home win in the Premier League under Julen Lopetegui as they beat promoted Ipswich Town 4-1 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Relief for Lopetegui as West Ham beat Ipswich

headlines.description: Anthony Gordon missed a penalty against his former side to spark an almighty roar from the home fans as Newcastle United were held to a 0-0 draw by Everton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gordon penalty woe as Newcastle held by Everton

headlines.description: Man United ground out a 0-0 draw at Aston Villa on Sunday in a Premier League game steeped in speculation about the future of manager Erik ten Hag.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ten Hag fails to inspire as Utd limp to Villa draw

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest earned a 1-1 draw at Chelsea on Sunday despite going down to 10 men in the 78th minute, as a fine defensive and goalkeeping display kept the hosts at bay in an at times frenzied Premier League encounter.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea winning run ended by 10-man Forest

name: Tottenham Hotspur at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion staged a storming second-half comeback with Danny Welbeck sealing a 3-2 home win over Tottenham Hotspur on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton storm back from 2-0 down to beat Spurs

name: West Ham United at Tottenham Hotspur

headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur came from a goal down to earn a comfortable 4-1 win over London rivals West Ham in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs thrash West Ham in 4-1 win; Kudus sees red

headlines.description: Aston Villa came from behind to beat Fulham 3-1 in a highly entertaining Premier League clash at Craven Cottage on Saturday in which the home side missed a penalty and both teams had a man sent off.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa into fourth after comeback win at Fulham

headlines.description: Manchester United bounced back after conceding a controversial goal to beat Brentford 2-1 at Old Trafford on Saturday for their first victory in more than a month.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd snap winless run with win vs. Brentford

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Newcastle United

headlines.description: Danny Welbeck's first-half goal against the run of play gave Brighton & Hove Albion a 1-0 win at Newcastle United on Saturday, a result that extended the home side's winless streak to four Premier League games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton punish wasteful Newcastle in 1-0 away win

headlines.description: Jordan Ayew scored in the 98th minute as Leicester staged a second-half comeback to earn a 3-2 win at Southampton in the Premier League on Saturday, with the 10-man hosts setting a club record of 21 matches without victory in the English top-flight.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leicester beat Southampton with 98th-min winner

headlines.description: Everton's Premier League mini-revival continued on Saturday as they comfortably beat a ragged Ipswich Town 2-0 away to make it four games in a row unbeaten, with the toothless hosts never looking likely to claim their first victory of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton continue revival with win at Ipswich

headlines.description: A brilliantly-worked Ryan Christie goal and a Justin Kluivert penalty gave Bournemouth a shock 2-0 Premier League win at home against 10-man Arsenal on Saturday, with William Saliba shown a straight red card in the first half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saliba sees red as Arsenal beaten by B'mouth

headlines.description: Manchester City defender John Stones scored a last-gasp winner in a 2-1 victory at Wolves on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: City edge Wolves with dramatic Stones winner

headlines.description: Curtis Jones struck Liverpool's winner in a 2-1 victory over Chelsea at Anfield to keep Arne Slot's side top of the Premier League table on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool stay top of Prem with win vs. Chelsea

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest striker Chris Wood profited from an error by Crystal Palace goalkeeper Dean Henderson to give his side a narrow 1-0 Premier League win on Monday that leaves the visitors still without a victory this campaign.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wood winner for Forest inflicts more misery on poor Palace

headlines.description: Chris Wood scored a brace as Nottingham Forest moved up to fifth in the Premier League with a 3-1 victory over hosts Leicester City in the East Midlands derby on Friday, underlining their excellent start to the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wood brace earns Forest derby win at Leicester

headlines.description: High-flying Aston Villa were held 1-1 by Bournemouth in the Premier League on Saturday thanks to a last-gasp glancing header from Evanilson.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Evanilson goal denies Villa important win

headlines.description: Two goals from Bryan Mbeumo saw Brentford fight back from a two-goal deficit to edge 10-man Ipswich  4-3 in an action-packed Premier League contest.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford beat Ipswich in seven-goal thriller

name: Wolverhampton Wanderers at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers' Matheus Cunha struck a superb stoppage-time equaliser to snatch a 2-2 draw at Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday as they came back from two goals down to grab a point that lifts them off the bottom of the Premier League table.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves draw at Brighton with late Cunha goal

headlines.description: Manchester City's Erling Haaland scored an early goal to take the champions provisionally top of the Premier League with a 1-0 victory over lowly Southampton at Etihad Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland helps City beat lowly Saints to go top

headlines.description: Substitute striker Beto headed a stoppage-time equaliser to earn Everton a 1-1 home Premier League draw with Fulham on Saturday after the Londoners had been seemingly cruising to a deserved victory courtesy of a goal by Goodison Park old boy Alex Iwobi.
headlines.shortLinkText: Beto header snatches draw for Everton vs Fulham

headlines.description: Goals by Nicolas Jackson and Cole Palmer earned Chelsea a 2-1 home win over Newcastle United in the Premier League on Sunday in the latest sign of progress for the Blues under coach Enzo Maresca.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palmer scores again as Chelsea hold off Newcastle

headlines.description: Crystal Palace beat Tottenham Hotspur 1-0 in the Premier League thanks to a Jean-Philippe Mateta goal to finally claim their first win of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace beat Spurs for first Prem win of season

name: Manchester United at West Ham United

headlines.description: Jarrod Bowen converted a last-gasp penalty to lift West Ham United to a 2-1 Premier League victory over Manchester United in a fluctuating clash between two struggling sides on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late VAR controversy sees Man United lose again

headlines.description: Mohamed Salah's late equaliser earned Liverpool a 2-2 draw with rivals Arsenal in an entertaining Premier League encounter at the Emirates on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal, Liverpool share spoils in title-rivals clash

headlines.description: Alexander Isak's early headed goal lifted Newcastle to a 1-0 victory over Arsenal on Saturday at St. James' Park, as Eddie Howe's men delivered a blow to Arsenal's title chase with their first win in six Premier League outings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Isak header puts huge dent in Arsenal's title hopes

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo and Evanilson got on the scoresheet as Bournemouth held on for a shock 2-1 victory over champions Manchester City on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: City lose unbeaten run in shock Bournemouth loss

headlines.description: Leicester City denied 10-man Ipswich Town their first win of the Premier League season on Saturday when a stunning second-half volley from left back Leif Davis was cancelled out by a late Jordan Ayew goal for the visitors.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Leicester equaliser denies Ipswich first win

headlines.description: A sensational trademark strike from Mohamed Salah earned Liverpool a 2-1 comeback victory over Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday, a win that moved Arne Slot's team two points clear at the top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah stunner in comeback win sends L'pool top

name: West Ham United at Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest made the most of the sending-off of West Ham United's Edson Alvarez with a rip-roaring 3-0 win on Saturday that lifted them into third spot the Premier League, with Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi and Ola Aina getting the goals.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest go 3rd as Wood scores in West Ham win

headlines.description: Promoted Southampton finally got to celebrate a first Premier League win of the season with a 1-0 victory over Everton thanks to a late Adam Armstrong goal.
headlines.shortLinkText: Southampton earn first win of season vs. Everton

headlines.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers remained winless and still bottom of the Premier League after an entertaining 2-2 draw at home to Crystal Palace on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves stay bottom after home draw with Palace

headlines.description: Two second-half goals from Dominic Solanke and a sublime James Maddison free kick helped Tottenham Hotspur come from behind to overwhelm Aston Villa 4-1 on Sunday and end the visitors' seven-match unbeaten run in the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Solanke shines as Spurs come back to thrash Villa

headlines.description: Moises Caicedo's blistering second-half strike cancelled out a penalty from Bruno Fernandes as Chelsea salvaged a 1-1 draw with Manchester United on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Caicedo equaliser earns Chelsea draw at Man Utd

headlines.description: A late double by Fulham substitute Harry Wilson snatched a 2-1 win over Brentford at Craven Cottage in the West London derby on Monday, with the winner coming in the seventh minute of stoppage time in their Premier League clash.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Wilson brace gives Fulham win over Brentford

headlines.description: Brentford striker Yoane Wissa netted a brace as his side twice came from behind to grab a 3-2 home win over Bournemouth in a rip-roaring mid-table Premier League clash on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wissa brace helps Brentford to win vs. B'mouth

headlines.description: Fulham cruised to a 2-0 victory over Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park in the Premier League on Saturday with goals from Emile Smith Rowe and Harry Wilson while the home side were reduced to 10 men when Daichi Kamada saw red for a dangerous tackle.
headlines.shortLinkText: Smith Rowe, Wilson shine in Fulham win vs. Palace

headlines.description: Everton ground out a point in a 0-0 draw at West Ham United on Saturday in a Premier League game in which neither side did enough to justify three points.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton grind out point at West Ham in stalemate

headlines.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers' fastest goal in their Premier League history from Pablo Sarabia set up a long-awaited victory as they beat fellow strugglers Southampton 2-0 on Saturday to move off the bottom of the table.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves bare teeth and sink Southampton for first win

name: Manchester City at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Manchester City lost 2-1 to Brighton & Hove Albion in the Premier League on Saturday, thanks to late goals from substitutes Joao Pedro and Matt O'Riley that gave the champions their fourth consecutive loss in all competitions.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton condemn Man City to 4th straight loss

headlines.description: Liverpool opened a five-point lead at the top of the Premier League as breakaway goals by Darwin Núñez and Mohamed Salah sealed a 2-0 win at home to Aston Villa on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool go 5 points clear with win over Villa

headlines.description: Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes bagged one goal and created two more in his team's spirited 3-0 Premier League victory over Leicester City on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Utd thrash Leicester as Amorim era set to begin

headlines.description: Newcastle United striker Joelinton scored his first goal since August as they came from behind to defeat Nottingham Forest 3-1 in their Premier League clash at the City Ground on Sunday, ending the fine run of their hosts in recent weeks.
headlines.shortLinkText: High-flying Forest beaten at home by Newcastle

headlines.description: Ipswich Town secured their first Premier League victory of the season when they deservedly beat Tottenham Hotspur 2-1 away thanks to first-half goals by Sammie Szmodics and Liam Delap on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ipswich stun Spurs to claim first win of season

headlines.description: Chelsea and Arsenal shared the spoils in a tense 1-1 battle at Stamford Bridge on Sunday, keeping the London sides level in the Premier League but still nine points adrift of leaders Liverpool.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea, Arsenal draw in lively London derby

headlines.description: Goals from Nicolas Jackson and Enzo Fernandez helped Chelsea to a 2-1 Premier League win at Leicester City on Saturday to climb into third-place in the table.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jackson, Fernandez net as Chelsea beat Leicester

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at AFC Bournemouth

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion beat Bournemouth 2-1 at the Vitality Stadium in the Premier League on Saturday with goals from Joao Pedro and Kaoru Mitoma, while the visitors were reduced to 10 men when Carlos Baleba was sent off in the second half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth vs Brighton: Pedro, Mitoma on target as visitors win

headlines.description: Arsenal returned to winning ways in the Premier League with Bukayo Saka and captain Martin Odegaard inspiring a comfortable 3-0 home victory against Nottingham Forest on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal thrash Forest to return to winning ways

headlines.description: Ross Barkley headed in a late equaliser as Aston Villa salvaged a 2-2 Premier League draw with lowly Crystal Palace on a wet and windy Saturday afternoon at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa vs Crystal Palace: Ross Barkley header saves draw

headlines.description: Ten-man Brentford picked up a first away point of the Premier League season following a 0-0 draw with toothless Everton at Goodison Park on Saturday, as the home side wasted numerous opportunities to score.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton held to scoreless draw by 10-man Brentford

headlines.description: Matheus Cunha's superb double earned Wolves a 4-1 comeback victory at Fulham in the Premier League on Saturday, extending the visitors' unbeaten run to four games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cunha's sublime double gives Wolves 4-1 comeback win at Fulham

headlines.description: Manchester City's misery continued with a shock 4-0 Premier League rout by Tottenham Hotspur at Etihad Stadium on Saturday, marking the first time in Pep Guardiola's dazzling managerial career that he has lost five games in a row across all competitions.
headlines.shortLinkText: City thrashed by Spurs for 5th consecutive loss

headlines.description: Premier League leaders Liverpool beat bottom side Southampton 3-2 on Sunday to build a sizeable eight-point lead over reigning champions Manchester City.

name: West Ham United at Manchester United

headlines.description: West Ham United won a Premier League game at Old Trafford for the first time in 18 years with a 2-0 victory over Europa League finalists Manchester United on Sunday thanks to goals in each half from Tomas Soucek and Jarrod Bowen.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United sink lower as West Ham earn 2-0 win

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest's hopes of securing a Champions League spot took a massive hit when they were held to a 2-2 draw by relegated Leicester City on Sunday, as Nuno Espirito Santo's side fell to seventh in the Premier League standings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest's UCL hopes hit by draw with Leicester

headlines.description: Eberechi Eze scored on either side of halftime to give Crystal Palace a 2-0 win at Tottenham Hotspur on Sunday and condemn the hosts to their 20th Premier League defeat of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Eze double hands Palace win, Spurs 20th EPL loss

headlines.description: Arsenal gave Liverpool a guard of honour and then a reminder of why they were the only team to challenge them in their romp to the Premier League title as they hit back from two goals down to draw 2-2 at a sun-drenched Anfield on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal honour Liverpool then fight back for draw

headlines.description: High-flying Aston Villa dispatched Tottenham Hotspur 2-0 in the Premier League on Friday to improve their chances of more Champions League football and leave their visitors firmly focused on next week's Europa League final.
headlines.shortLinkText: Villa ease past Spurs for big boost to UCL hopes

headlines.description: Chelsea stayed in the hunt for a Champions League place next season with a nervy 1-0 victory over Manchester United on Friday, courtesy of a goal from defender Marc Cucurella.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea close in on UCL with win over Man United

headlines.description: Everton gave Goodison Park a fitting farewell after Iliman Ndiaye scored twice to give the home side a 2-0 victory over already-relegated Southampton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton win to give Goodison Park fitting farewell

name: Nottingham Forest at West Ham United

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest kept alive their hopes of Champions League football next season with a 2-1 Premier League win at West Ham United on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest dream of UCL after 2-1 win at West Ham

headlines.description: Fulham beat Brentford 3-2 away in a roller-coaster Premier League encounter on Sunday to complete the double over their West London rivals.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wilson stunner sinks Brentford in 3-2 comeback win for Fulham

headlines.description: Jamie Vardy marked his final game for Leicester City with his 200th goal for the club as they beat fellow relegated side Ipswich Town 2-0 on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Vardy leads Leicester past Ipswich in final game

headlines.description: Arsenal snapped a season-long jinx against Newcastle United with a nervy 1-0 win in their last Premier League home game on Sunday all but securing runners-up spot and a place in next season's Champions League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal relief as Rice goal earns win vs. Newcastle

headlines.description: Substitute Jack Hinshelwood struck a late winner as Brighton & Hove Albion came from behind to grab a 3-2 victory over visiting champions Liverpool that keeps their slim hopes of playing in Europe next season alive after twice coming from behind.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton beat Liverpool to maintain European bid

headlines.description: Eddie Nketiah scored twice on Crystal Palace's party night as they celebrated their FA Cup triumph in style with a 4-2 victory over Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Nketiah and Eze on target on Palace party night

headlines.description: Manchester City gave Kevin De Bruyne a victory in his final appearance at Etihad Stadium, as Omar Marmoush, Bernardo Silva and Nico Gonzalez scored in a 3-1 Premier League win over Bournemouth on Tuesday that saw them climb to third in the table.
headlines.shortLinkText: City back in top 4 as KDB bids farewell to Etihad

headlines.description: Bournemouth secured ninth place in the Premier League, matching their previous best campaign, as Antoine Semenyo grabbed a double for a 2-0 home win over already relegated Leicester City on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Semenyo double ensures strong finish for Bournemouth

headlines.description: Manchester City's disappointing season ended with the consolation of a place in the Champions League after a 2-0 victory at Fulham on Sunday, earned by Ilkay Gundogan's overhead kick and an Erling Haaland penalty.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City secure UCL place with win at Fulham

name: West Ham United at Ipswich Town

headlines.description: West Ham United's Jarrod Bowen scored on his 200th Premier League appearance in a 3-1 victory at Ipswich Town as his club ended a disappointing season on a high note on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bowen gives West Ham final day win in 200th game

headlines.description: Liverpool signed off their Premier League title-winning season on Sunday with a 1-1 draw against Crystal Palace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool's season ends with 1-1 draw vs. Palace

headlines.description: Manchester United ended their disappointing season with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa on Sunday on the final day of the Premier League campaign, denying the visitors a Champions League qualification spot in the process.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United inflict costly defeat on Aston Villa

headlines.description: Newcastle United squeezed into the Champions League places despite losing 1-0 to Everton at home thanks to Manchester United's win over Aston Villa.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle secure CL spot despite Everton loss

headlines.description: Chelsea's Levi Colwill struck in the second half to send his team back to the Champions League with a breathless 1-0 victory at Nottingham Forest on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea see off Nottingham Forest in Champions League decider, clinch 4th

headlines.description: Southampton became the first team to lose 30 times in a Premier League campaign after they fell to a 2-1 defeat to Arsenal in the season finale.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal hand Southampton record 30th PL defeat

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion left Tottenham Hotspur with a hangover by thrashing the Europa League winners 4-1 away on Sunday but missed out on a European place after finishing eighth in the Premier League standings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brighton spoil Spurs' party with 4-1 win

headlines.description: Brentford were held to a 1-1 draw at Wolverhampton Wanderers on the Premier League season's final day on Sunday as Marshall Munetsi's sublime strike cancelled out Bryan Mbeumo's first-half goal.
headlines.shortLinkText: Brentford held 1-1 at Wolves after Munetsi thunderbolt
